title: PhysWorldLM Renderer Schema
description: PhysWorldLM World Specification Rendering Standard (PW-WSF-1200). Universal rendering configuration for any PhysWorldLM scene. Completely renderer-independent and simulator-independent. The renderer describes HOW the world is viewed — it never owns entities, materials, or transforms.

HDRColor.description: HDR color — components may exceed 1.0. Multiplied by intensity.

TransformReference.description: Reference to a transform defined in transform.schema.json or world.schema.json.

socket_name.description: Optional named socket or bone to attach to.

InlineTransform.description: Explicit world-space transform when not attached to an entity.

EntityReference.description: Reference to an entity in world.schema.json.

AssetReference.description: Reference to an asset in asset.schema.json.

MaterialReference.description: Reference to a material in material.schema.json.

LightReference.description: Reference to a named light within the renderer.

CameraReference.description: Reference to a named camera within the renderer.

RenderTargetBuffer.description: A single output render buffer / AOV.

RenderPass.description: A discrete rendering pass within a pipeline.

bias.description: Global LOD bias. Positive = use lower detail sooner.

force_lod_index.description: Force all objects to this LOD; omit to use automatic.

fov_vertical_deg.description: Vertical field of view in degrees (perspective).

ortho_width_m.description: Width in world units for orthographic cameras.

Viewport.description: A viewport defines one rendered view — camera + resolution + target buffers.

viewport_rect.description: Normalized screen rect [0,1] for split-screen or PiP layouts.

purpose.description: Semantic purpose of this viewport for AI and multi-output pipelines.

Light.description: A light source within the rendered scene.

temperature_k.description: Color temperature in Kelvin. Overrides color if set.

intensity.description: Luminous intensity (cd) for point/spot, luminous flux (lm) for area, illuminance (lux) for directional.

ShadowConfig.description: Shadow rendering configuration for a light.

weather_schema_ref.description: Reference to a weather.schema.json weather preset UUID.

ocio_config_ref.description: Path or URI to an OpenColorIO config file.

SceneVisibility.description: Controls which entities and assets are visible in this render configuration.

visible_entities.description: Explicit list of visible entity IDs. Empty means all entities are visible.

AIRenderTargets.description: AI and synthetic data render target configuration.

frame_count.description: Number of frames to render (offline/batch mode).

BackendHints.description: Optional backend-specific configuration blocks. Keys are backend names.

RendererIdentity.description: Identity block for the renderer configuration.

world_ref.description: UUID of the WorldSpec this renderer is bound to.

Renderer.description: Complete renderer configuration for a PhysWorldLM scene.

cameras.description: All cameras in this rendering configuration.

viewports.description: All simultaneous rendered viewports. Each viewport may have its own camera, resolution, and output targets.

standards.description: Declared standards and APIs this configuration targets.

extensions.description: Vendor/domain extension namespaces. Keys MUST be reverse-DNS strings.

examples.$comment: Example 1: Omniverse RTX Path Tracing — Photorealistic scene

identity.name: Omniverse RTX Path Tracing — High Fidelity

examples.$comment: Example 2: Multi-output AI Synthetic Data — RGB + Semantic + Depth + Normals simultaneously
Example 3: Military ISR — Thermal + EO + Radar simultaneous viewports
